items.body:
🔴 **Tool Issue** — `src/api/usageManagementContext.ts:37`

```ts
const userAgentInfo = `azsdk-js-arm-commerce/1.0.0-beta.1`;
```

The user agent string embeds `1.0.0-beta.1`, but the actual package version in both `package.json` and `CHANGELOG.md` is `4.0.0-beta.4`. This version mismatch is a generation tool bug — the context file was initialized with an incorrect version.

**Fix:** Update the user agent string to reflect the correct package version:

```ts
const userAgentInfo = `azsdk-js-arm-commerce/4.0.0-beta.4`;
```

Also report this in the [generation tool repository](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues) so it is not repeated in future regenerations.

1 tool issue found in `@azure/arm-commerce` (v4.0.0-beta.4): the user agent string in the generated context file (`src/api/usageManagementContext.ts`) is hardcoded to `1.0.0-beta.1` instead of the correct package version `4.0.0-beta.4`. The public API surface itself (rename of `OfferTermInfoAutoGenerated` → `OfferTermInfo`, introduction of `RateCardOperations`, `UsageAggregatesOperations`, `KnownVersions`, `AzureClouds`, etc.) shows no guideline violations — no broken client names, no re-ordered constructor parameters, no `unknown` type-safety issues, no missing exports, and no alpha versions anywhere.

<details>
<summary>📊 Structured Report</summary>

```json
{"agent":"mgmt-reviewer","pr":37597,"summary":"issues_found","findings":[{"file":"sdk/commerce/arm-commerce/src/api/usageManagementContext.ts","line":37,"issueType":"tool","category":"version-mismatch","description":"User agent string is hardcoded to azsdk-js-arm-commerce/1.0.0-beta.1 but package version is 4.0.0-beta.4"}]}
```

</details>
